comment.comment:
@Prasanto: Apologies for attempting to answer a Q you have asked Tarun. 

I think we should not have high expectations from a visit. We can't expect him to cure our problems.  He will make all the right noises: 
1) India is a great democracy
2) Mahatma Gandhi is great
3) Kashmir is a problem between India and Pak only
4) India and US have deep cultural ties
5) Make a good speech in the Parliament something he is good at and have our MPs failing head over heels to shake hands with him (as it happened when Clinton address them)

Things will largely continue the way they are. Its said that "Nations have no permanent friends and no permanent enemies. Only permanent interests" this is where as a country we fail. Obama will be here to serve the interests of US and not India. India needs to play the diplomacy game much better rather than expect such visits to be a game changer.
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

comment.comment: Play house Meena has already suggested. Reg schools when she is 2+ only options are La Marts, Modern High School and Sri Sri Academy. La Marts and MHS are automatic choices SSA is recommended in my view but its a new school and will be far (37A Alipore Road). When she is 3+ u cud try Birla High (now Sushila Bira), Ashoka Hall. Birla High is preferred if selected. Ashoka Hall mein ho jana chahiye. DPS and Heritage are other options but in my view last ones. Pratt Memorial has a good name but too academic hence not recommended.

comment.comment: "A very small portion of the electorate has access to social medias and can think objectively" R u suggesting that people who have access to social media are the ones who can think objectively. Only two times in history - once under Nehru and then once under the much condemned MMS has the incumbent Govt being voted back to power. See the recent power shift in UP a state in which large part of the population does not have access to Social Media. All these electoral  shifts do not happen due to the social mediaites as most of them hardly vote - (in the recent civic elections in Delhi only 31% pc people in Soiuth Delhi came out to vote that  too on a Sunday). In a democracy people should speak thru votes. Unfortunately even these the social media types want to outsource to likes of Anna Hazare and associates so that they can happily spend their weekends in the comfort of a mall.

comment.comment: Ketan Mayecha This issue is similar to change the captain and the team will win. NaMo his very presence is divisive he nomination as Election head  divided the BJP making LKA resign and broke the NDA with JDU walking away. Now two economists - Amartya Sen and Bhagwati Prasad - are fighting. see u and I who have never met are arguing. His battery of internet enthusiasts are going overboard with his projection. Look at the way Amartya Sen has been lynched (someone even wanting to take his Bharat Ratna back)  I have grave doubts that beyond the chattering classes (many of whom hardly vote) there is much support for him. In my experience hype has always led to disappointment (Facebook and Rel Power IPO are my fav examples). So i will like to be the child in the story "Emperor's new clothes" who did not get carried away the public opinion.

comment.comment: By banning u simply invite more attention to the subject.  The video is available all over the net for anyone to see and is being shared on social media. In effect in trying to prevent you have ended up giving it undue publicity.  In times we live censorship has no justification.  The govt must trust its people it is this same people  which gave it the mandate to govern.  If they had the maturity to reduce the ruling party to 44 seats give absolute majority for the 1st time since 1989 you got to trust them. Even otherwise the Govt should focus itself on more important things than banning documentaries and movies. One more thing whenever the right - BJP or the Left - left parties tmc etc come to power they do try to control the cultural agenda of the nation if it was possible they wud tell u what to wear what to eat what to read in short try and control every aspect of its citizen's life. This is not acceptable in a democracy. Let's come out of the medieval mindset and go with the times we live in

comment.comment:
Examine the following list.  What do all of these people have in common?

TS Eliot
Joe Biden, Jr.
Alex Haley
James Cameron
George Harrison
Helen Keller
Michael Jackson
Madonna 
Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.
Osama Bin Laden
Michael Bolton
Dan Brown
Dr. Jane Goodall 
Stephen Ambrose
Doris Kearns Goodwin
James Frey
Kaavya Viswanathan
Vladimir Putin 
 if you answered that they are all plagiarists, you're right. They've all used the words or ideas of someone else and passed them off as their own in at least one instance.  
This trivia itself is picked up from this site. 
http://wrnotebook.blogspot.in/2013/04/down-plagiarism-rabbit-hole.html?m=1

comment.comment: The reason why global brands don't come from India needs to be understood. In India we identify the largest of institutions with individuals - Tata with tatas, Kolkata reliance Ambani, congress with Gandhis, Infosys with nayananmurhty (he is pulled out of retirement to mentor it once again) and I can go on. Look at the other side Microsoft has gone beyond the gates, and Apple is doing a fine job without Jobsz even Google has a professional ceo.  Now see  our profession from this perspective  almost all the firms in India are inseparable from their owners and therein lies the problem and that is what stops them from being perpetual and growing beyond a point. Rather than invest in systems and processes to ingrain perpetuity into the firm the firms are managed like a family owned business (which is in line with the rest of India Inc) and what brought them the success in the 1st place (the key partner) becomes a bottleneck in the practice going global. I welcome comments on my thought process

comment.comment: Under 10th Schedule (Anti-Defection), exemption is given to the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker. Paragraph 5 of 10th Schedule states that a person who has been elected to the office of the Speaker or the Deputy Speaker shall not be disqualified. So the positon of the speaker is not the same as a regular party member.

comment.comment: Firstly the media which is responsible for making it known to all the people and secondly its the parents who incessantly went on discussing it in the name of child education/protection. We play into the hands of the media and pay the price. This whole thing deserved a royal ignore but in name of being educated and informed parents we have ensured that most children know ab it. This fancy about the medium is a dangerous the fact that someone died or was molested is least important - whether blue whale game was involved or whether the culprit was a uber driver or whether the people involved met over Facebook these things become more important. The way the media has been feasting since yday over the death and violence looming it directly into our home is more dangerous than any of these games. I am writing little incoherently as there is a seething anger inside on the foolishness of the people and the height of irresponsiblity of the Indian media. But for the these 2 factors these kids wud  not have known anything about this game.

comment.comment:
Swami vivekanda did not have money to travel to Chicago to attend the parliament of world religions, read details below 

Raja Ajit Singh of Khetri just then had a son. He invited the Swami to bless the child and told him that he will bear the expenses of his ticket.

With funds collected by his Madras disciples, the kings of Mysore, Ramnad, Khetri, diwans and other followers, Narendra left Bombay for Chicago on 31 May 1893 with the name "Vivekananda" which was suggested by Ajit Singh of Khetri. The name "Vivekananda" meant "the bliss of discerning wisdom".

Buddha's begging bowl is legendary he was rich by virtue of being born in a royal clan but his enlightenment, philosophy and his whole life as a teacher has nothing to do with his rich royal background.

Coming to Chanakya read this from his Wikipedia page where he goes all the way to patliputra to attend an alms giving ceremony. 
One day, the king DhanaNanda organized an alms-giving ceremony for Brahmins. Chanakya went to Pupphapura (Pushpapura) to attend this ceremony.

So vivekananda didn't have money to travel to america, Buddha begged throughout his life and chanakya went all the way to attend alms giving ceremony yet what srk is correct for srk is an honourable man (with due apologies to shakespeare/mark antony).

comment.comment:
Would like to mention a few things
1) Most of the budget allocated both in state and central for things like education, medical, better sewage systems, clean water etc remains under utlised. Therefore money is the not the problem. It is Public Finance so we cannot apply the same logic which applies an individual/pvt organisation
2) Till 2018 this statue was not built/completed and therefore THIS money was available but not utilised. The point is it is a futile way to think that THIS money could have been better utilised for other things. 

The issue is not money but application, someone took the initiative to create something gigantic, got the funds allocated and accomplished the task in a time bound manner and deserves kudos for it rather than the logic that THIS money could have been better used this way or that way.

comment.comment: The problem my friend is not in the knowledge any more. we spent the entire last century addressing the knowledge gap and have resolved it wonderfully every knowledge (actually information) in the world is available at the press of a button. The real challenge we face today is what is called the duryodhana's (actual name was Suyodhan) dilemma where he says I know what is the right thing to do but still I am not able to do it. I think our challenge in this century is to address the execution gap not only knowing what is right but also being able to to do what is right without falling for the many internal compulsiveness that we have. The starting point for this can be being able to wake up when u want without snoozing the alarm without listening to yr mind (which will tell 'kal se kar lenge from tom pakka) and to our body (I need some more rest today/kitna late se soya tha etc) .

comment.comment:
Payal Agarwal Lohia & I went thru a lot of thought on this and I am thankful that Payal was convinced that we should go for a second child.  If our parents had also thought that bringing up a number of kids was a challenge many amongst us would not be writing these posts on FB. Each child comes with his/her own destiny. 

It is my humble request to parents that not to take parenting so seriously, as a task, as some kind of to do - it is none of it. Parenting should be like gardening - create the right environment, provide the manure, water, fertilisers, prune the weeds and the fruits/flowers will blossom. Please pardon my saying this but it is not the job of the gardener to pull the flower/fruit out of the seed which is what I see happening in many cases of parenting.

comment.comment: Let me introduce (to those who are unaware of) Pritilata Waddedar, a female martyr from Bengal. Born in Chittagong, she was educated in Chittagong, Dacca (now Dhaka) and Calcutta (Kolkata), and graduated with Philosophy, with distinction. In September 1932, dressed as a Punjabi male, she led a group of 15 men and torched âPahartali European Clubâ which had a signboard that read, âINDIANS AND DOGS NOT ALLOWEDâ. She was successful in her attempt and the club was destroyed. British officers chased the group and fired on the saboteurs, including Pritilata. In the ensuing gun-battle, she was fatally wounded but she refused to surrender, and helped many from her group escape (they were later caught and punished). When she saw no possibility of escaping, Pritilata, rather than being caught by the British, consumed cyanide and died. She was 21. Where is her story?

comments_v2.title: Pawan Lohia commented on Roopendra Narayan Roy's post.

comment.comment:
If only they had consulted Hayek India would have been saved from these opinions. While receiving the award Friedrich August von HAYEKâS in his speech at the Nobel Banquet on December 10, 1974 said 
.... 
Yet I must confess that if I had been consulted whether to establish a Nobel Prize in economics, I should have decidedly advised against it........ 

It is that the Nobel Prize confers on an individual an authority which in economics no man ought to possess.

This does not matter in the natural sciences. Here the influence exercised by an individual is chiefly an influence on his fellow experts; and they will soon cut him down to size if he exceeds his competence.

But the influence of the economist that mainly matters is an influence over laymen: politicians, journalists, civil servants and the public generally.

There is no reason why a man who has made a distinctive contribution to economic science should be omnicompetent on all problems of society â as the press tends to treat him till in the end he may himself be persuaded to believe.

One is even made to feel it a public duty to pronounce on problems to which one may not have devoted special attention.

I am not sure that it is desirable to strengthen the influence of a few individual economists by such a ceremonial and eye-catching recognition of achievements, perhaps of the distant past.

I am therefore almost inclined to suggest that you require from your laureates an oath of humility, a sort of hippocratic oath, never to exceed in public pronouncements the limits of their competence.

Or you ought at least, on confering the prize, remind the recipient of the sage counsel of one of the great men in our subject, Alfred Marshall, who wrote:

âStudents of social science, must fear popular approval: Evil is with them when all men speak well of themâ.

All his fears have come true. Full speech here 
https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/economic-sciences/1974/hayek/speech/
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

comment.comment:
@sonali my complain/suggestion is similar to what tagore said in his poem bangamata reproduced below in Bengali (with english font) and with translation.

Bongo Maata

Punye paape dukkhe shukhe potone utthaney

Manush hoite dao tomar shontaney

Hey sneharto Bangobhumi, tobo grihokrore

Chiroshishu kore aar rakhiyo na dhorey.

Desh-deshantor majhe jar jetha sthaan

Khunjiya loitey dao koriya shondhan.

Podey podey choto choto nishedher dorey

Bendhe bendhe rakhiyo na bhalo chele korey.

Pran diye, dukkho shoye, aponar haathey

Shongraam koritey dao bhalo mondo-shathe.

Sheerno, shaanto, sadhu tobo putroder dhorey

Dao shobe grihochara, lokkhichara korey.

Shaath koti shontanere, he mugdho jononi,

Rekhecho Bangali korey, manush koroni.

Mother Bengal

Through good deeds and wrong, in sorrow and happiness, downfall and uprise,

Allow your children to flourish and grow, to full human potential realise.

Oh affectionate mother, in your lap most comforting â

As children you must not keep them forever nursing.

Across the world, wherever their places may be

To truly find themselves, you must set them free.

With a hundred little rules that cannot be understood

Do not force blind conformity for just the sake of being good

They must learn to stake life and face torment strong

To find out how to take the right along with the wrong

Grab your good natured sons and push them out there

The world is theirs for the taking, let them show no fear

This great multitude of children, Oh doting mother

Confining them to a Bengali identity, you have not let them discover the greater in humanity.
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

comment.comment:
"Otherwise the TMC statement about Marwaris would not have upset you" I am honestly not upset on it so let us not presume things it is just a political ploy to divide the non-minority votes. Some nondescript fellow saying something on a channel makes no difference, I can assure you that most Marwaris are least affected and the people who have rushed to raise a bogey are doing so out of their own political compulsions or them holding a certain position as head of some organisation.

"if he would have written the same lines today or tweaked it" yes he would have added a 'keno' to it rekhecho bangali kore manush (keno)  koroni'  

"For Bengalis made homeless and uprooted by Partition and subsequent commubal violence leading to every kind of loss and yet contributing in every field and enriching the nation" who is denying that but again to associate that to a particular identity and to say therefore whatever absurd political choices they make are justified I beg to differ.

" I remain and will remain a proud bengali" no issues again but then in the name of pride don't stop people from pointing out the fallacies like wrong political choices, fudging of data, the ill-conceived Global Advisory Board the botched handling of corona.

"we as a community have done well." show me one comment where I have denied that but can u say the same about West Bengal. Why are we mixing up the two?

"If the Congress did not deliiberately suppress the bengalis causing other ripple effect in bengal the story might have been far brighter." I don't think in the history of independent India there has been a greater collective effort to suppress anyone than Modi and yet he became CM repeatedly and then PM twice so let's not blame others for no one can suppress anyone if he/she is resilient enough remember Pranabda was a prominent figure at the Centre from 1980 to 2012 and then as the 1s Citizen of the country from 2012 to 2017 and then Bharat Ratna (that too under BJP). Though I always wonder why the people of the state do not take as much pride in him as they do in worshipping false gods (apologies to Arun Shourie) like Sen and Banerjee.

As u have rightly said we could go on and on essentially saying the same thing from different perspectives. I will close by saying that we should listen to the last 2 lines of Tagore in the poem that I have reproduced above.

comments_v2.title: Pawan Lohia commented on ULTIMATE ASTROLOGER's post.

comment.comment:
1) Btwn belief and knowledge - knowledge is a no brainer. 

2) Belief can make u do ridiculous things. My God is the best is a belief and root cause of many religious problems/fight/quarrel. 

3) The knowledge that each one have their own God (the basis of Sanatna Dharma) frees u from the bondage of my God vs yr God. 72 hoors, swarg are examples of belief.

4) Faith (trust in God/Almighty/Supreme Power) is the ultimate knowledge and frees you from all bondages and enables you to accept the vagaries of life with equanimity. In short, Faith is knowledge in practice.

The above are my original submissions and subject to corrections/debate/improvements/amendments.

comment.comment:
There is a difference between asking for concession and asking for an exemption. Parents need to understand that what they are paying to school is not good enough if all costs are considered on opportunity cost basis. Kolkata mein jyadatar badi schools ko agar complex/mall mein convert kar diya jaye the owners will make more money from it then they can make from schools in next 10/20 years. Ek fashion sa ban gaya doctors or schools ko badnaam karna. 

I am asking u did the school forcing u to take admission. I am surprised to see ki Kolkata ki one of the costliest schools mein padhane wale parents ko fee maafi chahiye aur sara kam dhanda chodh kar pichle 2/3 mahine se lage hue is muhim mein. Aaj bhi jyadatar schools ki monthly fee aapke driver ki salary se kam hai. 

Anyway, i am opting out of these arguments. Enjoy the kick that stoking this popular sentiment brings.

comment.comment:
One needs to und the socio economic and political considerations behind maintaining petrol prices at certain levels. 
1. Today of govt reduces the price bcoz global prices are down tom when the rates go up it wil be socially and politically v difficult to raise the prices again. 
2. Also the cross subsidy on kerosene needs to be absorbed. 
3. If prices are reduced and consumption goes up forex outflow will suddenly go up. 
4. States hv a major role to play as major part of the price is sales tax and it's a major source of revenue for states. 
So there are a host of logical and sensible reasons for maintaining oil prices within a certain range.

comment.comment:
Some overriding Qs

1) will the people of the state be sensible enough to vote out a govt which has been a worse extension of the regime they voted out in 2011. 

2) Will the people of Bengal finally learn to make sensible and practical political choices and get Bengal into mainstream rather than being a rebel without a cause it has been since the 40/50 years. 

3) Will Bengal become free from political violence like rest of India (forget prosecution not even arrest happens for a political murder in Bengal both parties blame each other and the matter ends). 

There is a lot Bengal needs to think today what rest of the world/india has already thought and done.

Bengal - if thou shall do it (in 2021) thou shall grow
if not fates with rebels (without a cause) do conspire. 
(due apologies to Shakespeare)

comment.comment:
On the contrary I find majority of media stars (rajdeep, shekhar gupta, abhishar sharma, ravish, sagarkia, barkha dutt, entire NDTV to name a few).
The country seems divided bcoz like the pre social media (SM) era some few cannot control the media and set the narrative. If SM was not there the media giants would have made a Demi-God out of Rahul Gandhi like they had done for Sanjay (maruti)/Indira (Durga, Indira is india)/Rajiv (desh mein computer kaun laya), Sonia (sactifice drama and her NAC ruling the country by proxy). comment.comment:
Pupa (2018)

The Indrasis Acharya directorial is based on the subject of euthanasia or mercy killing and perhaps the first one to approach the subject, ever since the Supreme Court recognised passive euthanasia. It revolves around the life of a Bengali living in the US whose father slips into a coma. This unfurls the dilemma of whether or not he should stay in India. The hard-hitting family drama won the Best Film award at the Hanoi International Film Festival along with several other awards in India.

Love one another, but make not a bond of love:Let it rather be a moving sea between
the shores of your souls.
     Fill each otherâs cup but drink not from
one cup.
     Give one another of your bread but eat
not from the same loaf.
Sing and dance together and be joyous,
but let each one of you be alone,
     Even as the strings of a lute are alone
though they quiver with the same music.

     Give your hearts, but not into each
otherâs keeping.
     For only the hand of Life can contain
your hearts.
     And stand together yet not too near
together:
     For the pillars of the temple stand apart,
     And the oak tree and the cypress grow
not in each otherâs shadow.

comment.comment:
Shrimati Draupadi Murmuâs personal life is a story of resilience and âself-prideâ. There was an unforgettable moment for her in 2009 when her 25-year-old son Lakshman died unexpectedly after attending a gathering in Bhubaneshwar. His death broke her. Supriya Kumari, head of the Brahma Kumari ashram in Rairangpaur, Odisha says, âShe was completely shattered. She wasnât having any life within her to even talk.â

In one of the talks on Brahma Kumariâs television programme, Draupadi Murmu herself narrated this event, âThe tsunami came into my life in 2009. It was a huge jolt to me. I couldnât hear anything for a few days. I slipped into depression. Log kehte the ye to mar jaegi (People thought I will not survive). But, no, I wanted to live.â

After two months, she visited Supriya Kumari in the Brahma Kumaris ashram, completed a course and learnt Sahaj Rajyog. She recovered by changing her life. Since then, *she gets up every day around 3.30 am* and retires to *bed by 9.30 pm*. She *does yoga and meditation unfailingly and is punctual as well*. The spiritual bent of life not only survived her but stabilized her too.

But tragedy befell Murmu again when her younger son, Shipun, died in a road accident. When his body was brought home, she was broken completely, once again.

Rajesh Sharma, a local journalist who was present at her home, says, âShe was crying uncontrollably. She broke down before her sonâs body. She raised her hands to the sky, asking, âGod, what more do you want from me? What is left now?ââ The catastrophe came in droves.

In an indescribable series of events, her mother and a younger brother died within a month. And a year later, due to deep depression, her husband, Shyam Charan Murmu, died as well.

At that point, Droupadi Murmu told a TV anchor with pain in her voice: âWhen my second son died, the jolt was a little less than before because I was doing meditation. My husband wasnât as strong as me, so he couldnât survive.â She insisted on her only daughter, Itishree, to marry and lead a normal life. *After the deaths of five family members, she turned to spirituality and vegetarianism*. When she was the governor of Jharkhand (2015-2021), she made the kitchen completely vegetarian. She is likely to bring many fundamental changes in the running of the government in the Rashtrapati Bhavan if she becomes the President.

Draupadi Murmu has *donated her familyâs land in Pahadpur for public causes*. She runs the SLS residential school in memory of her husband and two sons. At the exact location, she has made samadhis in their memory. Itâs a heart-wrenching sight.

At the same time when you see tribal girls and boys getting free education and decent surroundings around samadhis, you can visualise carving out a beautiful future out of a deathly past.

Take a bow Droupadi Murmu, to be the President of India!
~~~~Forwarded~~~~

comments_v2.title: Pawan Lohia commented on Samir Patodia's post.

comment.comment:
Ajit Doval spent seven years in disguise while working as an undercover agent in Pakistan. His target was Dawood Ibrahim and Pakistanâs spy agency ISI. He had mastered Urdu and is an expert on Pakistani history, culture and politics. 

Negotiating with IC 814 Hijackers

In 1999, terrorists hijacked Indian Airlines IC 814 with 155 passengers and took the aircraft to Kandahar in the Taliban-ruled Afghanistan. It is said that Ajit Doval had negotiated with the hijackers and brought the Indian nationals and aircraft safely. India had to release three dreaded terrorists.

Release of 46 Indian Nurses

In 2014, Doval played a crucial role when 46 Indian nurses were trapped in Iraq's Tikrit Hospital. He ensured their safe release. Doval flew to Iraq as a part of a top-secret mission. He contacted the hierarchy in the Iraqi government and convinced the ISIS terrorists to hand over the nurses safely to the authorities at Erbil City.

Surgical strike in Myanmar 

In 2015, Doval and the then Army Chief Gen Dalbir Singh Suhas had planned a cross-border military operation against the National Socialist Council of Nagaland insurgents, operating out in Myanmar. In the operation, the Indian Army troops managed to kill over 35 insurgents. However, the Myanmarese government denied the strikes. 

Uri Surgical strike 

In 2016, Indian Army troops carried out a surgical strike in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir and inflicted significant casualties on the other side of the Line of Control. The strike was carried out in response to the attack on the Uri military base of the Indian Army in which 19 soldiers were killed. Doval is said to be the architect of this strike. 

Balakote strike 

In 2019, the Indian Air Force carried out the Balakote airstrike in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir and destroyed the terrorist training camps. The airstrike was conducted in response to the Pulwama terror attack in which 40 CRPF soldiers were killed.

I also write against people who take a monk to the cleaners for his wrong utterances against swamiji but keep quiet on the political violence in the state and then when to come to voting go and elect the same regime again and again. Reg Mr sen I hv mentioned facts none of which are disputed I repeat his credentials are high but his conduct is lowly.

Regarding criticising Bengalis in general people who know me personally will vouch that nothing is farther from truth. I take as much pride in bengal as anyone here does but don't want to rest on the past laurels. Yes i truly believe that just bcoz netaji, gurudev, swamiji et al were born in this state does not add an iota of greatness to the commoners in the state. This sense of pride because of these luminaries is a bane for our people and sooner this is understood the better it is.

Modi govt is doing a great job of showing them their rightful positions. They shud feel lucky that in spite of what they do they are allowed to roam free. Media Gossip: As per Kolkata Maidan grapevine, a couple of days back the Publisher and E-in-C of a 'national' daily (which had a change of guard recently) convened the senior editorial team for a 'green-field' off-site and told them that he had himself stopped reading the paper for quite sometime as it had been turned into a tabloid. He went on to present to the team several Market Research Reports on the opinion of readers and advertisers. He then recommended a complete revamp of editorial 'style' (not necessarily content) and design. He reportedly said something to this effect (apparently in as many words): it is fine to be critical of the powers-that-be or take an anti-establishment line without turning a paper into a pamphlet for a few claps on social media.  (Reported as over-heard in a bar - so cannot vouch for veracity)

comment.comment:
This reminded me of the post below I wrote recently during ram mandir inaugural.

Krishna is known and reverred for so many things he did 

Ram is reverred for so many things he did not do

1. At the time of vanvas Dasharath asked him to revolt against him and take the throne - Ram didn't 

2. Laxman asked him to use his bow and arrow to stop the injustice meted out to him - he didn't 

3. His nanihal was very close to the jungle where he was staying never took any support from them

4. Bali had ravan under his belt, ram could hv taken his help and got Sita back but he rather killed bali as bali had forcibly overtaken his brother sugriv's wife and taking help of such a person would be against dharma - so Ram didn't take Bali's help

5. When sita was kidnapped all he needed to do was ask Bharat for help but he was in exile and it was his personal battle - Bharat was not even aware of the battle ram was fighting - didn't take help of his own brother 

6. After killing of bali and coronation of sugriv (around the month of June) the army was ready and willing to move to lanka but ram stopped it saying that monsoon had set in and waited for 4 months for chaumasa (rainy season) to get over - Ram ka dhairya

7. Could hv overtaken the kingdom from sugriv and vibhishan but didn't and made them kings. Till date India is the only major country which has never attacked or overtaken any other country. 

It is this quality of ram of doing the right things and avoiding shortcuts which we worship.

Look forward to yr feedback/review/comments

comment.comment:
I remember in 2016 the Posta under construction flyover had fallen and every Kolkatans heart was aching due to the enormity of the man made tragedy.

During the same time India was playing the semi final of the T20 world cup and the final was to happen in eden gardens. It was the only time in my life that there was a sort of relief when India lost the semi final for it wud hv been really difficult to cheer for India in the aftermath of such a humungous tragedy.

As the citizen of a nation or state or city or society we share a collective conscience which at times demands that we put aside our personal joys and revellries aside and adhere to call of this common conscience to be the sufferers of the tragedy whom we may know but being human shud be above all.

comment.comment: Roopendra Narayan Roy yes sir I am doing it. the way the SC has intervened and diluted the entire matter granting another relief to West Bengal govt. We all know what the judges say during hearing is good for the gallery but has no legal impact unless it's part of the order. In 30 years the Hon'ble judge has not seen such a case and yet their is nothing at all in order which addresses it. The day the SC had taken suo motto cognisance I had written that they will dilute the issue and that's exactly how the Cookie has crumbled and this is not the 1st time. The Calcutta high court passes an order the SC dilutes it. The way the CJI recently introduced Kapil Sibal to a Brazilian delegate it seemed Sibal and CJI and DYC is some junior. The way CJI has allowed KS and AMS to dominate the matters in SC is a matter of shame for judiciary. So you are absolutely right but it's not I but the duo of  KS and AMS which is Cocking a snook at the judiciary.

comment.comment:
Sir
Being an EY alumnus have been following this matter very closely and I don't think the response has been that of a 'weak and poor leader'. (refer this post of EY India chairman on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/posts/rajivmemani_many-of-you-will-be-aware-of-the-tragic-demise-activity-7242552793239080960-jUw0?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_android)

Having worked for EY for 13 years I can vouch for its humane approach. What has happened is most unfortunate and no one attending the funeral was an unexpected thing which the chairman has admitted multiple times.

I am not defending EY just bcoz its my alma mater but bcoz of my long and close association I am privy to the DNA of the Organization and while what has happened is tragic and response shud hv been better it does not reflect on the overall culture. It's a growth oriented org but is not inhuman as is made out to be.

comment.comment:
Rajib Kumaryes no one wants to end up with an egg face.
From 22 they went up to 29 without popular support u mean to say. In 2014/16/19/21/24 all seats of howrah and Kolkata (the urban seats) hv gone to the incumbents - u mean all are rigged I refuse to believe.
Reg minorities it's a foregone conclusion across India (Kashmir being latest example) but what about others what about those who oppose in public and vote for them in private.
The state in which the state is today is due to the foolish voting patterns of the people of bengal since 1977 specially the urban voters - always anti establishment, rebel without a cause opposing the party at the Centre be it congress earlier and bjp now.
Unless the people (specially voters of kol/Howrah belt) change their voting pattern nothing will change. Oppose in public but vote for them in pvt has to stop at a mass level.
Forget the masses even the likes sourav Ganguly and Aparna sen who hv been bestowed with everything and have nothing to fear - even they follow the same pattern. The rot runs deep and has been there for too long.

Sorry for a long post on Navami morning. Both u and I and people like us want the good of the state and want our state to prosper economically but can't ignore hard facts and follow the habit of blaming everyone else (the centre, the bjp, the SC, the election commission, cbi/Ed almost everyone under the sun) except the voters who consistently vote them to power. Stand by what i hv said and keep saying - the fault dear Bengal is not with our stars but solely lies with the voters that our state is a laggard (apologies to Shakespeare).

comment.comment:
Regarding passion sharing a note I had written some time back addressing this very issue

Bhagwad geeta does not support passion (do what u like) 

Any work that a person enjoys doing regularly with full involvement even if there is no incentive attached to it - the world call it his/her passion. 

Now drawing a corrolary out of it

If a person can do everything with full involvement on a regular basis even if there is no incentive attached to it - the world will call him an intense person. This is called nishkam karma in bhagwad geeta and as said by krishna in chapter 12 shlok 12 

If u can't do anything do abhyas (practices) 
Knowledge is higher than practices
Higher than knowledge is meditation 
And higher than meditation is nishkam karma. 

A great way to succeed is to find yr passion and being able to monetize it. Examples are sachin tendulkar, srk,  

But even higher than above is being passionate about whatever u do which implies doing it with full/complete involvement irrespective of likes or dislikes and whether it is remunerative or not. Best examples are MS Dhoni, Modi, Big B

comment.comment:
Got reminded of this note I had written the day after I got fractured some months ago

Time is not a resource which needs to be managed nor a commodity which needs to be used. 

Time is life itself and life is valuable because it is limited. How you spend yr time is how u live yr life. 

And currently most if not all of us are rushing thru our lives trying to pack more and more action into less and less time and this quest is endless. We are even teaching our children to do the same. Technology which was supposed to an enabler for saving time has been converted into a tool for achieving more in less time rather than enjoying the time it saves. 

This has also to do with our economic model which is based on unlimited growth. For the world to survive it has to keep growing economically and the responsibility for this growth has been subtly passed on to people are economically well off.

Its time to rethink the concept of time, growth, to really think if less is more, to reflect upon if gratification is happiness. I don't know what the correct answer is but I know for sure that the model most people are following currently needs a serious revisit.

Take time to live for u never know when it's time to leave
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

Manoj Mohankain 1974 this prize was awarded to economist fredreich hayek and this is what he rightly said

FEE.org

All Commentary
Tuesday, October 11, 2022
Abolish the Economics Nobel Prize
Why Hayek (and I) would've opposed the creation of a Nobel Prize in economics.

Image: Nobel Peace Center (iStock)
Economics
Banking
Economics
Experts
Nobel Prize
Scientism

Peter Jacobsen
In 1974, the Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel (colloquially known as the Nobel Prize in economics) was awarded to Austrian economist Friedrich Hayek.

Hayek doubted whether such an award was a good idea.

âI must confess that if I had been consulted whether to establish a Nobel Prize in economics, I should have decidedly advised against it,â he said.

Hayek provided two reasons for opposing the prize. First, he expressed concern that the prize encourages âswings of scientific fashion,â though he joked this problem must not be too bad given how unfashionable his own ideas are.

Hayekâs opposition rested primarily on the second reason he gave.

âThe Nobel Prize confers on an individual an authority which in economics no man ought to possess,â Hayek observed

Mr Sen is an example of the Fears that hayek expressed.

comment.comment:
Ramayan and mahabharat are two epics which perhaps Hv the greatest influence on  our country our lives our culture. In Ramayan thr battle was fought following the dharma and hence no rules were broken but mahabharat was opposite every rule was broken but Krishna ensured that Pandav are not the first one to break the rules.

In our life also we come across battles where dharma is followed and where rules (dharma) are not being followed. We accordingly hv to decide our strategy. The battle of adharma cannot be fought with dharma that's the key msg of Krishna when he asks Arjun to kill unarmed Karan and when Karan questions Krishna He reminds him off everything from what he called Draupadi in Rajyasabha to the immoral killing of abhimanyu of which karan was a part.

In summary let's try and fool ourselves into believing that when one side across globe is fighting the battle with all sorts of adharma the other side can safeguard itself with dosti and aman ki asha. The battle of adharma cannot be fought with dharma. The sooner as a society we understand this better for all of us. And let me add to this that - de dee hamein azadi bina khada g bina dhaal - is the biggest lie we hv been made to believe in and with passage of time this shall will get uncovered to more and more people. It was the navy mutiny of 1946 at Bombay (also called the last battle of independence) which got us independence and not the Quit India movement which happened before the WW2 in 1942 and lost all its relevance after WW2.

There is much more that can be written but in interest of brevity I must pause

comments_v2.title: Pawan Lohia commented on Bhaskar Nath's post.

comment.comment:
Krishna is known and reverred for so many things he did 

Ram is reverred for so many things he did not do

1. At the time of vanvas Dasharath asked him to revolt against him and take the throne - Ram didn't 

2. Laxman asked him to use his bow and arrow to stop the injustice meted out to him - he didn't 

3. His nanihal was very close to the jungle where he was staying never took any support from them

4. Bali had ravan under his belt, ram could hv taken his help and got Sita back but he rather killed bali as bali had forcibly overtaken his brother sugriv's wife and taking help of such a person would be against dharma - so Ram didn't take Bali's help

5. When sita was kidnapped all he needed to do was ask Bharat for help but he was in exile and it was his personal battle - Bharat was not even aware of the battle ram was fighting - didn't take help of his own brother 

6. After killing of bali and coronation of sugriv (around the month of June) the army was ready and willing to move to lanka but ram stopped it saying that monsoon had set in and waited for 4 months for chaumasa (rainy season) to get over - Ram ka dhairya

7. Could hv overtaken the kingdom from sugriv and vibhishan but didn't and made them kings. Till date India is the only major country which has never attacked or overtaken any other country. 

It is this quality of ram of doing the right things and avoiding shortcuts which we worship.
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

comment.comment:
Sandip Kunduhere is the piece i mentioned 

Flight musings

How are you?? 

How are you is an important part of a conversation. Its generally the ice breaker but with passage of time like our good morning greeting how are you has also become salutary and emotionless. Many a time we don't even wait for the other person to answer it or ask it in such a mechanical way that the other person would rather not answer it then to tell you how he/she may be. This is an outcome of imparting etiquettes as a training rather than imbibing them. 

Next time do not teach your child to say thank you, teach them to feel grateful, practice gratitude on a daily basis and next time the thank you will be genuine and from the heart rather than 

beta aunty ko thankoo bolo. Haan very good boy. 

And then we blame our children 

Coming back to our original discussion on how r u. 

Trust me if you ask mukesh ambani genuinely and compassionately - how are you sir - he will say dhando mando chhe share price down chhe beta ka vyah mein bahut kharcho hoi geyo. 

I will not repeat the mistake by telling you how to say how are you but next time u do it please do it with compassion and love and mean it. Try it for a week gauge the results yourself. 

Flight has landed signing off

Pawan Lohia

comment.comment:
There are 3 forms of knowledge 

1. Acquired knowledge earned by reading hearing seeing translating to degrees
2. Experiential knowledge earned by doing called experience 
3. Realised knowledge earned by thinking, meditating, contemplating, reflecting called spiritual knowledge

You had certain experience which you are trying to get answers to by acquired knowledge. My humble suggestion try the realised knowledge route. The knowledge of others will reduce your thirst for true knowledge but yr own realised knowledge will quench it.

comment.comment:
Good Q and here is the answer

The boon: Gandhari was granted a boon by sage Vyasa to have 100 sons.

The frustration: Her pregnancy lasted two years, and in her impatience, she struck her womb, at which point she gave birth to a mass of flesh.

The solution: Sage Vyasa instructed her to place 101 pieces of the flesh (100 for sons and one for a daughter, as she requested) into 101 pots filled with ghee.

The birth: After a period of time, the Kauravas were born one by one from these pots, with Duryodhana being the first.

comment.comment:
Panchali Singh Anand this happens when we form an opinion even without watching anyway sharing the story and the video

The movie Dhurandhar does not feature the real P. Chidambaram as a character, but it contains a storyline that alludes to real-life allegations involving a former Indian Finance Minister and a fake currency scam. 
The film depicts a scenario where an Indian minister and his son allegedly help sell sensitive national assets, specifically currency printing plates or high-quality currency paper, to Pakistan's intelligence agency ISI, which then uses them to infiltrate India with high-quality fake currency to destabilize the economy. 
This plot point is widely interpreted as a reference to real-world controversies and allegations that surfaced during the Congress-led UPA government's tenure when P. Chidambaram served as the Finance Minister.

https://www.instagram.com/reel/DSIQN3Oj3x5/

Collect all the facts this movie has uncovered (most of the characters and events have real life connection) and then may be re-watch it u will understand why the entire leftist liberal pro pak literati in India is so rattled with this movie.

Good luck

comment.comment:
Bhagwad geeta chapter 12 shlok 12 krishna says
Better than mechanical practice is knowledge; better than knowledge is meditation. Better than meditation is renunciation of the fruits of actions, for peace immediately follows such renunciation.

The hierarchy is

1. Practice (action/karma)
2. Knoweldge (gyan)
3. Meditation (dhyan)
4. Nishkam karm/bhakti (devotion does not exactly capture the essence of bhakti or Nishkam karma)

Now using #2 to understand #4 is like asking a graduate to check PhD paper. It won't work. For most educated logic (gyan) has become the be all and end all of everything but both dhyan and bhakti are above and beyond logic and can only be realised experientially. Also the 1st two - Karm and bhakti there is a cause and effect relationship but in case of dhyan and bhakti there isn't any such correlation of cause and effect. Just to explain the difference between karma and Nishkam Karm

A child's carektaker/aaya  and the mother both perform the same sets of actions vis a vis the child but one is karma and other is bhakti. The quality of action decides whether its karma or bhakti.

Will take a pause in interest of keeping the post short. More of it some other day.

comment.comment:
One of the most important lessons life teaches us is this: the world is not fair and life is not fair.
Even in countries where systems function relatively well, ordinary people still feel wronged. Fairness is an aspiration, not a permanent reality.
We must strive for fairness. We must work towards justice, integrity, and better systems. But at the same time, we must accept a deeper truth: fairness, like perfection, is a journey, not a destination.
People will cheat. Power will be misused. Corruption will exist. No system has eliminated this. Neither the democracies of India or the United States nor the autocratic structures of China or Russia have created absolute fairness.
Different systems, same human nature.
The world improves slowly because people keep trying. Yet, by and large, life remains uneven, unpredictable, and imperfect. In my view understanding this is not pessimism, It is maturity

comment.comment:
Well the numbers tell a different story though, consider this

1. Bjp got 45.85 pc votes
2. Tmc got 40.8 pc votes
3. Bjp got 32 lacs votes more than tmc
4. Compared to 2021 extra votes cast were also 32 lacs (5.99 cr in 2021 vs 6.31 cr in 2026)
5. This was one the cleanest election bengal has ever seen so the 40.8 pc vote share  is genuine
6. A mere shift of 2.5 pc (16 lac votes) may hv changed the course of the election
7. Had bjp/modi/shah done one thing less (SIR,  CRPF, EC, ED CBI, killing IPAC, choking funding sources, booth level control, not attacking Mamata, jhalmuri, amit shah sitting in bengal for 15 days and it's a long list) a few things here and there and tmc wud be ruling us again

It is true that the truth is coming out now but it really took humungous effort by modi/shah/ec/sc/crpf/bjp to bring bengal to this stage and in spite of all this 40.8 pc still voted tmc.

We were just a whisker away from being ruled by tmc against and Amit shah deserves a bharat ratna for making bengal independent again
comment.author: Pawan Lohia

comment.comment:
Sir

As u always say facts are sacred so let's stick to pure facts

1. BJP got 45.84 pc votes
2. Tmc got 40.8
3. In absolute terms bjp got 32 lacs votes more than tmc
4. In 2021 599 lacs votes were cast in 2026 631 lacs which is 32 lacs more were cast
5. If just 16 lacs votes had moved from bjp to tmc the results could have been totally different and similar to Kolkata wherein spite of such a one sided election - Chowringhee, Balygunj, Entally Beleghata all voted for TMC
6. If we take Howrah and Kolkata combined TMC has won more seats than BJP in what is otherwise a totally one sided election and if rest of Bengal followed their path TMC would still be in power. In Kolkata + Howrah region tmc got 14 seats and BJP 13 

And see the quantum of actions that had to be taken to bring votes of Bengal out of their stupor (SIR, Para Military forces, BJP getting its own house in order, Amit Shah sitting in bengal for 15 days, managing SC from the clutches of Sighvi and Sibal, TMC own hubris and share of mistakes (ekta community jokhon, makka madina, 4 may dupur 12ta DJ, folding up of IPAC, financially constraining TMC by using ED/CBI/Income tax and my list has 105 such action points) 

So while we all are extremely happy that we got rid of the devil/evil and now that facts of their wrongdoings are pouring out everyday lets not forget that 
1. even in a free and fair election with such a large participation 40.8% still voted for TMC 
2. in Kolkata + Howrah TMC got more seats than BJP 
3. some of the elite seats of Kolkata - Ballygunj, Chowringhee still voted for TMC in large nos, Jadavpur saved the day.

comment.comment:
It's not Hindus. It's any group whose primary identity sits above the nation:
â¢ Religion (e.g., Islamist politics) â¢ Political ideology (e.g., communism) â¢ Regional or historical nationalism (those who still push the "bohiragoto" narrative and blame outsiders for Bengal's decline) â¢ Linguistic nationalism (seen in parts of Tamil politics)
Bengal unfortunately has strands of all four.
Some may argue that Hindu identity also falls into this category. The difference is that for most Hindus, national identity comes first. In the examples above, religion, ideology, region, or language often take precedence over the nation itself.

comment.comment:
It's the change of circumstances not the mind. Whatever is happening in bengal from grassroot level party worker to mla and MP shows the lust for power (satta) in all and sundry. It is this powerlust at such a widespread level that makes bengal different from most/all other states. In no other state is politics a source or employment at such a large scale (from local people to tollywood to cricketers to painters to artists to almost everyone resorts to politics as a means of earning and employment even the most successful icon of this century from Bengal could not resist it) 
It will be interesting to see if BJP yields to the local trend or tries to change it
